headlines.title: AI is coming ... for the job listings
headlines.description: New data from job board Indeed finds the number of jobs that have listings with “AI” in the title have tripled from 2022 to 2026, from less than 3% to more than 8%.

headlines.title: Officials Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting in Maine as Fallout Grows
headlines.description: There is a growing demand for answers after a fatal ICE-involved shooting in Maine. According to an ICE spokesperson, the shooting happened when agents tried to conduct a traffic stop and, they say, the vehicle tried to get away. That’s when ICE says an agent opened fire “fearing for public safety.” The incident, which was partly captured on video, is the second fatal shooting involving ICE agents in the past week. NBC’s Erin McLaughlin reports for TODAY. Warning: Some of the video may b

headlines.title: Trump holds bilateral meeting with Iraqi Prime Minister
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as President Trump holds a bilateral meeting with the Iraqi prime minister amid tensions rising in the Middle East.

headlines.title: E. Jean Carroll collects more than $5 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
headlines.description: E. Jean Carroll has collected her $5 million judgment against President Donald Trump, more than three years after a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

headlines.title: NASA tests jets for supersonic flight
headlines.description: NASA is testing the X-59 aircraft with technology for flight that’s faster than the speed of sound that doesn’t create a loud sonic boom. NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz spoke with NASA test pilots James Less and David Nils Larson about what it’s like to fly the jet and some of the technology behind it.

headlines.title: The future of public safety may start with a drone
headlines.description: Brinc Drones supplies 20% of U.S. SWAT teams in 50 states. NBC News' Gadi Schwartz spoke with the company's 25-year-old founder.

headlines.title: Get a Rare Look Inside Levi’s Denim Research Lab
headlines.description: In 1873, Levi Strauss and Company crafted their first pair of blue jeans and the brand has defined American style for 150 years. The company’s first female CEO, Michelle Gass, sits down with TODAY’s Savannah Sellers to discuss the company’s legacy and evolution, speak out on the use of artificial intelligence and share at a look at archival pieces worn by icons like Beyoncé.

headlines.title: How Much of Social Media Health Trends Are Backed by Science?
headlines.description: Health trends on social media promise to fix everything from sleep to weight gain but how much of that advice is backed up by science? NBC’s medical contributor Dr. Akshay Syal joins TODAY to separate fact from fiction on trends like issues with high cortisol, berberine supplements for weight loss, Japanese walking and more.

headlines.title: Top Summer 2026 Tech Trends That Will Make Your Life Easier
headlines.description: Tech expert Katie Linendoll joins TODAY to share the latest tech trends to make your life just a little bit easier this summer. New gadgets and apps include the Stickerbox, SPARQ AI that diagnoses your car for issues and more.

headlines.title: How to Be a Tech-Savvy Traveler to Save Time and Money
headlines.description: Long lines, crowded airports and high gas prices can all make trips challenging but with a little planning, there are ways to save time and money. NBC’s Brian Cheung joins TODAY to share how to become a tech savvy traveler. Tips include using digital IDs, getting deals from credit card travel portals and more.

headlines.title: New Warning About Fake Invitation Scam: Here’s What to Know
headlines.description: The FTC is warning about a new came of fake party invitations making the rounds in your emails and texts that could be phishing for your personal information. NBC’s Vicky Nguyen joins TODAY to break down how to protect yourself and spot bogus RSVPs.

headlines.title: Flock's A.I.-enabled street cameras see backlash as more communities cancel contracts
headlines.description: Activists are speaking out against new, A.I.-enabled cameras being used by municipalities across the country. Critics argue the technology is creating a mass surveillance network that can be accessed by federal immigration agencies. NBC News' Dana Griffin reports.
